title: Proposal: new AccessAction 'acquire' for buyer-providing-payment semantics
body:
Filing per the KNYT rep/rewards/tasks decisions doc §11.B and the operator's 2026-05-10 sign-off.

## Problem

The KNYT rep/rewards/tasks workstream wants every gated action to flow through evaluateAccess, including direct purchases (a buyer paying KNYT to receive entitlement to an asset). The spine's current AccessAction set works well for everything EXCEPT this case:

- 'read' / 'watch' / 'listen' — read-side; ownership check
- 'invoke' — generic side-effect; reward-eligibility checks land here
- 'mint' / 'transfer' — token lifecycle; sync receipt; gates on ownership
- 'payment-settle' — value settlement; sync receipt; gates on ownership

For 'mint' / 'transfer' / 'payment-settle' on a payment-gated descriptor, the spine's payment-gated branch in evaluateAccess returns:
  - allow=true reason='owned' — if the persona ALREADY owns the asset (correct for post-purchase audit)
  - allow=false reason='payment-required' — if not (correct for read-side denial CTA)

Neither is right for a buyer who is CURRENTLY providing payment. They don't own it yet (so 'owned' is wrong); they aren't being denied (so 'payment-required' is wrong).

## Today's workaround (B.2 commit 0bfef906)

The /api/purchase/complete route does its existing payment validation + entitlement grant, THEN calls evaluateAccess(persona, descriptor, 'payment-settle') AFTER the entitlement is in user_entitlements. The spine sees the post-grant state, returns allow=true reason='owned', and emits a sync receipt. We discard the decision and keep the receipt side-effect.

This works but is semantically awkward: the receipt anchors 'persona owns the asset' rather than 'persona just bought the asset'. The audit reader has to infer purchase semantics from timestamp proximity to the OrchestrationEvent.

## Proposal

Add a new AccessAction:

  type AccessAction = | ... existing ... | 'acquire';

Semantics:
  - Persona-providing-payment-to-receive-entitlement
  - In TX_CLASS_ACTIONS (sync receipt, FIO-required guard)
  - Evaluator branch:
      - if descriptor.gating.kind === 'free' → allow reason='free'
      - if descriptor.gating.kind === 'credential' → run external resolver (cohort/token), allow if matches
      - if descriptor.gating.kind === 'payment' → allow reason='acquiring' (NEW reason)
        — the gate's role here is to verify identifiability + FIO + credentials, NOT ownership
      - existing payment-validation stays in the consumer (purchaseHandler)

Required additions:
  - AccessDecisionReason: add 'acquiring'
  - SYNC_RECEIPT_ACTIONS: add 'acquire'
  - Decision tree in evaluateAccess.ts for the 'acquire' branch (skip userOwnsAsset check)

## How the workstream would consume it

  const decision = await evaluateAccess(persona, descriptor, 'acquire');
  if (!decision.allow) return { error: decision.reason };  // fio-handle-required, credential-required, etc.
  // proceed with payment validation + entitlement grant
  // sync receipt already anchored

This matches the spine's 'single decision authority' principle without the post-grant re-evaluation hack.

## Scope

This is a spine-canonical change — touches services/access/evaluateAccess.ts, services/access/policyResolvers.ts, types/access.ts. Per CLAUDE.md's canonical-files ban, I'm filing it for spine-team review rather than implementing it from this workstream.

Not blocking: the post-grant receipt pattern in B.2 works end-to-end. This is the cleaner long-term shape.
